name: TuRBo (3BLD)
description: TuRBo is an advanced 3BLD (3x3 blindfolded) solving method created by Erik Akkersdijk in 2007. It solves two pieces at a time using U-layer setups, providing a systematic approach that sits between beginner methods (like Old Pochmann) and expert methods (like BH/3-style). TuRBo offers faster execution than setup-based methods while requiring fewer algorithms than full commutator approaches.

steps.name: Memorize Edges and Corners
steps.description: Memorize the cycle sequences for all edges and corners. The memo is structured to work with TuRBo's two-at-a-time solving approach, where pieces are paired for simultaneous solving.
tips: Use letter pairs for efficient memorization
Memo structure is similar to other BLD methods
Practice fast memo techniques independently from execution
Ensure parity detection during memo phase

steps.name: Solve Edges Two at a Time
steps.description: Solve edge pieces two at a time by setting up target pieces to the U layer, then applying short algorithms that swap two edges simultaneously into their correct positions. This is more efficient than solving one piece at a time.
tips: Learn the U-layer setup moves for all edge targets
The two-at-a-time approach significantly reduces total move count
Practice edge pair solving until setups become automatic
Handle odd-number edge cycles with an extra single swap

steps.name: Solve Corners Two at a Time
steps.description: Solve corner pieces two at a time using a similar U-layer setup approach. Corners are set up and swapped in pairs using short algorithmic sequences.
tips: Corner setups are generally simpler than edge setups
Practice corner pairs separately before combining with edges
Handle corner twist cases that may arise
Parity between edges and corners may require an additional algorithm

pros: Faster than Old Pochmann and M2/R2 methods
Two-at-a-time solving reduces total algorithm count vs. one-at-a-time
Good stepping stone toward learning full 3-style/BH
Systematic U-layer setups are relatively easy to learn
cons: U-layer setups can be lengthy for some targets
Not commonly used at the very top competitive level
Fewer community resources compared to Old Pochmann or BH
creator: Erik Akkersdijk
